rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim is circular and weak ('the contribution is the protocol description above'); checking whether the thesis, gap statement, and claimed contribution are coherent is plausibly central under any reading of 'check this'.
rationale.R1.2: The protocol has non-trivial logical gaps — it is not established how the leader lease preserves linearizability under concurrent reads, and the leader-election rule (highest sequence number) is underspecified for split-brain scenarios; consistency checking is plausibly load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative claims (87k ops/sec, 40% improvement, 3.1 ms median latency) are asserted without describing the experimental setup, cluster conditions, or confidence intervals; verifying claim-evidence alignment is plausibly load-bearing.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation 'Check this.' is equally consistent with a writing-quality review; prose-level checking is therefore elevated to central until the intent is clarified.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on technical correctness — checking whether the protocol logic is sound and whether the experimental data actually supports the performance claims — or on writing and presentation quality?
